rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+)
One exercise = one bounded activity
Student agency — adults decide
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Split a mega-exercise when phases are distinct skills with distinct artifacts
Artifact-realism check
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
Classroom is default delivery; body teaches the work, trainer manages the room
Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 15: '{{prompt:compound-and-close-1}}' — prompt generates ./CLAUDE.local.md content; no forced-engagement mechanic inside the fence (e.g. 'for each rule I added, confirm the session moment or remove it'). Body prose adds push-back instruction outside the fence; speed-runners skip body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-line forced-engagement step to compound-and-close-1's fence, e.g. 'For each rule you add, name the session moment that earned it. Remove any rule you can't ground in a specific moment.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points + UX — file must be both plug-able and operable
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes
Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch
rules_evaluated.evidence: Compound phase watch-fors present (retro confabulation + Nerd push). Ticket phase has no failure-mode + escape-hatch row — dominant failure (connector install yak-shaving) is named in a body callout but not in the structured maintainer block format rule 47 requires.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a ticket-phase row to maintainer watch-fors naming: failure = connector install exceeds timebox; escape = trainer calls 'path 3 only' and moves on.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 15: '{{prompt:compound-and-close-1}}' — self-review shape carries known RLHF self-charity bias (Claude over-rates its own session read, names moments without quoting). No body callout adjacent to fence names this bias or offers a harsher alternative.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a callout before or after the compound prompt naming the self-charity risk and a paste-ready harsher alternative, e.g. 'quote the line or remove the rule'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout, not forcing function
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
